hypothesis: Incorporating DFT+U corrections specifically parameterized for the problematic 3d transition metals (Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni) as an intermediate-fidelity level within a multi-fidelity ∆-learning cascade—where the first ∆ corrects GFN1-xTB to DFT+U and the second ∆ corrects DFT+U to HSE06—will yield lower mean absolute errors in predicted band gaps for transition-metal-containing MOFs than the current single-step GFN1-xTB-to-HSE06 ∆-learning model, because the hierarchical correction will decouple the strong-correlation error from the remaining exchange-correlation error, making each individual correction more learnable from the available descriptor set.
